name: Pressure Cooker Pork Chops
recipeIngredient: 4-6 potatoes, peeled and sliced into somewhat thick pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat your pressure cooker on medium-high heat and add enough oil to coat the bottom.
2. Once hot, brown the pork chops until they are dark but not burned. This helps render better gravy and enhances flavor.
3. Ensure you brown all sides of the pork chops as much as possible before removing them to a plate.
4. Reduce the heat to medium and add the sliced potatoes (watch out for hot grease). Pour in enough water to cover the potatoes.
5. Stir the potatoes to coat them thoroughly, then add the chopped onion without stirring.
6. Place the chops on top of the potato mixture and season with salt and pepper if desired.
7. Put the lid back on the pressure cooker. When the "bell" rocks, set a timer for 20 minutes.
8. After cooking, allow the cooker to cool naturally by running water over the top.
9. Carefully remove the pork chops and potatoes to your serving plate. The chops will be very tender, and the potatoes will be infused with gravy.
10. Serves 4.
Note: This is not a fast recipe as it takes time for sufficient pressure to build at medium heat. Increasing the heat may risk burning the potatoes.
